paperTitle: AgentVista: Evaluating Multimodal Agents in Realistic Scenarios
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "AgentVista: Evaluating Multimodal Agents in Realistic Scenarios".

Key contribution: Real-world multimodal agents solve multi-step workflows grounded in visual evidence. For example, an agent can troubleshoot a device by linking a wiring photo to a schematic and validating the fix with online documentation, or plan a trip by interpreting a transit map and checking schedules under routing constraints.

## When to use

- Apply when solving agentvista problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.23166
